leagueBriefing: The First Four concluded in Dayton with a night defined by historical milestones and perimeter dominance. Miami (Ohio) capped an incredible 32-1 campaign by securing its first tournament win in 27 years, while Prairie View A&M stifled Lehigh to earn the program's first-ever NCAA Tournament victory. Both 11 and 16 seeds now move on to face basketball giants Tennessee and Florida, respectively.

games.headline: Miami (Ohio) silences skeptics with record-setting barrage to sink SMU
games.brief: The RedHawks proved they belonged in the Big Dance, riding a historic shooting performance to dispatch SMU in Dayton. After an undefeated regular season was followed by a disappointing conference tournament exit, Miami (Ohio) relied on a First Four-record 16 three-pointers to overwhelm the Mustangs' size advantage. Eian Elmer led the way with 23 points, including a punctuation-mark dunk in the final minute that secured the school's first tournament win since 1999 and set up a date with Tennessee.
bullets: Eian Elmer sparked the offense with 23 points, shooting 6-of-9 from beyond the arc.
Miami (Ohio) set a new First Four record by knocking down 16 total three-pointers.
The win marks the first NCAA Tournament victory for the RedHawks in 27 years.
SMU's Jaden Toombs posted a double-double with 20 points and 11 rebounds in the loss.
The RedHawks finished the night matching the Mustangs in rebounding (35-35) despite being significantly undersized.
games.context: Miami (Ohio) advances to the Round of 64 to face No. 6 seed Tennessee in the Midwest Region.

games.headline: Panthers defensive masterclass earns Prairie View A&M first tourney win
games.brief: Prairie View A&M turned a defensive clinic into school history, suffocating Lehigh's top scorers to claim the program's first-ever NCAA Tournament win. Despite trailing at halftime, the Panthers used a decisive 14-2 second-half run to pull away, led by a 25-point outburst from Dontae Horne. The story of the night was the Panthers' length and athleticism, which held Lehigh's star Nasir Whitlock to just five points on 2-of-15 shooting and forced 16 turnovers.
bullets: Dontae Horne led all scorers with 25 points and added four steals in the victory.
Prairie View A&M's defense limited Patriot League MVP Nasir Whitlock to just 5 points.
The Panthers recorded 9 blocks and 12 steals to disrupt the Lehigh offense.
Hank Alvey provided a bright spot for Lehigh, recording 23 points and 15 rebounds.
This victory represents the first NCAA Tournament win in Prairie View A&M program history.
games.context: Prairie View A&M advances as a No. 16 seed in the South Region to face No. 1 seed Florida.
